{"id":"project-LICHEN-7tss","title":"UdpDatagram.to_bytes payload length overflow","description":"File: python/src/lichen/ipv6/udp.py:63-77

The UdpDatagram class does not validate that the payload fits in the 16-bit length field:

```python
def to_bytes(self, src: IPv6Address, dst: IPv6Address) -> bytes:
    ...
    without_checksum = (
        self.src_port.to_bytes(2, 'big')
        + self.dst_port.to_bytes(2, 'big')
        + self.length.to_bytes(2, 'big')  # length is UDP_HEADER_LENGTH + len(payload)
        + bytes(2)
        + self.payload
    )
```

The `length` property (line 59-61) returns `UDP_HEADER_LENGTH + len(self.payload)`.

If `len(self.payload) > 65527`, then `length > 65535`, causing:
```
OverflowError: int too big to convert
```

Impact:
1. Confusing error (OverflowError instead of UdpError)
2. No early validation - error only occurs at serialization

Fix: Add validation in to_bytes or a __post_init__ method:
```python
max_payload = 0xFFFF - UDP_HEADER_LENGTH  # 65527
if len(self.payload) > max_payload:
    raise UdpError(f'payload too large: {len(self.payload)} > {max_payload}')
```

Tags: correctness, ipv6","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:06.871833Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:06.871866Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-7tss","title":"UdpDatagram.to_bytes payload length overflow","description":"File: python/src/lichen/ipv6/udp.py:63-77

The UdpDatagram class does not validate that the payload fits in the 16-bit length field:

```python
def to_bytes(self, src: IPv6Address, dst: IPv6Address) -> bytes:
    ...
    without_checksum = (
        self.src_port.to_bytes(2, 'big')
        + self.dst_port.to_bytes(2, 'big')
        + self.length.to_bytes(2, 'big')  # length is UDP_HEADER_LENGTH + len(payload)
        + bytes(2)
        + self.payload
    )
```

The `length` property (line 59-61) returns `UDP_HEADER_LENGTH + len(self.payload)`.

If `len(self.payload) > 65527`, then `length > 65535`, causing:
```
OverflowError: int too big to convert
```

Impact:
1. Confusing error (OverflowError instead of UdpError)
2. No early validation - error only occurs at serialization

Fix: Add validation in to_bytes or a __post_init__ method:
```python
max_payload = 0xFFFF - UDP_HEADER_LENGTH  # 65527
if len(self.payload) > max_payload:
    raise UdpError(f'payload too large: {len(self.payload)} > {max_payload}')
```

Tags: correctness, ipv6","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:06.871833Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T23:39:05.635760883Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T23:39:05.635760883Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T23:44:05.635760883Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T23:39:05.635760883Z"}

new_value: Added max_payload validation in to_bytes(); raises UdpError for >65527 byte payloads (prevents OverflowError). Test passes.
